children.name: Bertram de Verdun of Great Moulton

zh.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton於1145年之前與de Verdun of Moulton在艾歐洛結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton于1145年之前与de Verdun of Moulton在艾歐洛结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Moulton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Cheshire and Lancashire): habitational name from one or more of the places called Moulton (Cheshire, Lincolnshire, Norfolk, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire, Suffolk, North Yorkshire), most of which are named with the Old English personal name Mūl(a), the Old Norse personal name Múli, or Old English mūl ‘mule’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’.

variants.name: Molden
meanings: metronymic from the Middle English female personal name Mold, a form of Maud, with the characteristically West Midland genitival suffix -en.

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

nameMeanings.name: Verdun
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French: habitational name from any of several places in France called Verdun. The placename is probably of Gaulish origin, deriving from ver(n) ‘alder’ + dūn ‘hill, fortress’.
Spanish and Catalan: habitational name from Verdú in Catalonia (Spain), which has the same origin as the French name (see 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Verdon  , a cognate of 1 above. This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Virden
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Verdun  .

variants.name: Verdon
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, in origin a Norman baronial name from any of various places so called from Gaulish elements meaning ‘place of alders’ (  compare  Vere  ).

variants.name: Varden
meanings: Anglo-Norman: Wardeyn, a guard, a keeper of a gate

en.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton married de Verdun of Moulton before 1145, in Europe. They were the parents of at least 2 sons.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton a épousé de Verdun of Moulton avant 1145, à Europe. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton hat vor 1145 in Europa de Verdun of Moulton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton lei sposato de Verdun of Moulton prima del 1145, nel Europa. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli.

ja.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moultonは1145年以前にヨーロッパでde Verdun of Moultonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton(은)는 1145년 이전 우럽에서 de Verdun of Moulton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton casou-se com de Verdun of Moulton antes 1145, em Europa. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton вышла замуж за человека по имени de Verdun of Moulton перед 1145 г. в местности Европа. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs de Verdun of Moulton se casó con de Verdun of Moulton antes de 1145, en Europa. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos.
